mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and accelerating inflation indicates an overheating economy where corporate earnings growth outpaces discount rate pressures, generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in quarterly regression.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply chain constraints, leading to negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of declining INDPRO months across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in consumer prices triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of high-inflation months, or signal IC turns positive.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue baselines, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or payroll growth shows no predictive power for quarterly returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and falling housing starts indicates a shift in consumer spending toward services and away from durables, creating sector rotation that depresses broad market returns.
failure_condition: Broad market returns are positive in more than 60% of divergence episodes, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A severe monthly contraction in industrial production signals acute supply-side bottlenecks that disrupt corporate earnings forecasts, triggering immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of severe contraction months, or signal fails to predict drawdowns.
